event: 67 years old
female
presented to a local hospital complaining of backache
calculi in both kidneys associated with ureteral calculi
underwent HLL combined with double J catheterization
purulent urine
severe fever (body temperature 39.5 °C)
associated with chill
low blood pressure (60/42 mmHg)
blood and urine samples were sent to the laboratory for culture
empiric anti-infection drugs were given
fluid resuscitation (30 mL/kg)
a vasoactive agent (Norepinephrine)
Hydrocortisone sodium succinate hormone
symptoms progressively deteriorated over the next 48 h
anuric
diagnosed with ARDS
placed on invasive ventilatory support with endotracheal intubation
CRRT
transferred to the ICU of our hospital
blood pressure 80/62 mmHg
heart rate 139 beats/min
transcutaneous oxygen saturation 80%
cold, clammy extremities
a loud bubbling sound in the lung
oxygen partial pressure 50 mmHg (oxygen concentration 1.0)
lactic acid 10.6 mmol/L
bicarbonate 10.8 mmol/L
central venous pressure 20 cmH20
large area of B lines was monitored in the lung using bedside ultrasonography
diffuse dysfunction, severe functional damage in the left ventricle
left ventricular ejection fraction (LVEF) 20.3%
normal function in the right ventricle
sinus tachycardia associated with broad ST depression
significantly elevated troponin I (10.2 ng/mL)
amino-terminal brain natriuretic peptide precursor increasing beyond the maximum normal range (>35,000 pg/L)
creatinine 356 μmol/L
anuria
multiple organ function failure (e.g., heart, lung, and kidney failure)
VA-ECMO treatment was urgently initiated
ECMO venous leading-out end used a 20F catheter
ECMO arterial leading-in end used a 17F catheter
catheters were placed in the left femoral vein and right femoral artery, respectively
No. 6 arterial catheter was placed in the right femoral artery
ECMO (centrifugal pump Maquet Rotaflow RF 32) ran normally 3000 rpm, blood flow 3.5 L/min, arterial pressure 135 mmHg, venous pressure −29 mmHg
Norepinephrine (1.0 μg/kg/min) and Epinephrine were stopped
bedside CRRT was performed
Imipenem and Cilastatin sodium combined with Vancomycin were used for anti-infective therapy
the lactic acid level had declined
vasoactive drugs were completely stopped
the arterial blood lactate level had reached the normal range at 12 h
extended-spectrum β-lactamase-positive Escherichia coli was found in both blood and urine cultures
inflammatory indices (e.g. leukocytes, calcitonin, C-reactive protein) diminished
the minimum serum trough concentration of Vancomycin was monitored, and it remained within the range of 15--20 μg/mL
LVEF was 35%
black and necrotic skin was found at the extremities of both lower limbs and the end of a finger on the right upper limb
successfully weaned from ECMO therapy on day 6
renal function was scaled as acute kidney injury grade 3
bedside CRRT was discontinued
the treatment time of each session lasted 10--12 h
the patient began urinating, with its volume gradually increasing to a maximum of 1000 mL/day
vascular ultrasonography and computed tomography angiography were performed in the bilateral lower limbs
necrotic tissues of the lower extremity were amputated
tracheal intubation was used for respiratory function support
spontaneous breathing was successful, but weaning failed
the anti-infection combination of imipenem and Cilastatin sodium and Vancomycin was replaced by Cefoperazone sodium and Sulbactam sodium
bedside physical rehabilitation was started
she was weaned from the ventilator on day 20 after admission
the tracheotomy tube was sealed on day 25
the patient was discharged successfully on day 32, although she continued intermittent hemodialysis
at the 6-month follow-up, the patient declared that she could take care of herself, although she still required intermittent hemodialysis
